items.title: 2FA response manipulation
items.description: Server trusts client-side success flag in JSON response after OTP step.
items.how_to_find: Intercept the 2FA verification response in Burp. If body contains `success: false`, replay with `success: true` while keeping cookies from the login step.
items.how_to_fix: Never trust client-supplied success flags. Validate OTP server-side and issue a new session only after server-side verification. Bind 2FA state to server session, not response JSON.

items.title: 2FA status code manipulation
items.description: Application treats HTTP 200 as auth success regardless of body content.
items.how_to_find: Send invalid OTP; note 401/403 response. Use Burp Match/Replace or manual edit to return 200 OK with the same failure body.
items.how_to_fix: Authorization decisions must parse response body and server-side OTP state. Reject on invalid code regardless of status code tampering from client.

items.title: CAPTCHA token reuse
items.description: Same CAPTCHA response token accepted multiple times.
items.how_to_find: Solve CAPTCHA once, capture token parameter, replay on subsequent login or registration requests without solving again.
items.how_to_fix: Single-use CAPTCHA tokens bound to session and action. Server-side verify with provider; invalidate after use.

items.title: CAPTCHA disabled via parameter removal
items.description: Backend skips CAPTCHA validation when parameter omitted.
items.how_to_find: Remove `g-recaptcha-response`, `captcha`, or honeypot fields from request and submit login/registration.
items.how_to_fix: Require CAPTCHA verification on all sensitive flows. Fail closed if token missing or invalid.
